mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through improved profit expectations without requiring monetary accommodation.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 357841M as of Jan 2006 reflects sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% and remains elevated, the cost of capital rises, reducing the present value of future equity cash flows. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.16% as of Dec 2005 indicates restrictive monetary stance
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day returns is positive and > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals weakening labor demand and reduced consumer income, which depresses aggregate demand and corporate earnings. This interaction between employment weakness and demand contraction produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS declines > 0.2% MoM, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances, or the joint predictive power (Rank IC) of the PAYEMS-RSAFS pair falls below 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 0.3% month-over-month indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power, supporting corporate profit margins and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices earnings upward.
failure_condition: When INDPRO grows > 0.3% MoM, SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 5-day returns is negative or < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1900K units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness. Combined with elevated federal funds rates, this interaction produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market anticipates demand contraction.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2123K as of Nov 2005, FEDFUNDS at 4.16%
failure_condition: When both HOUST < 1900K and FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC is < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, which depresses discretionary spending and corporate earnings. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as growth expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% as of Dec 2005, near the trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market weakening or early recession
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 5.0%, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances, or Rank IC between UNRATE level and 20-day returns is positive and > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% year-over-year, combined with elevated FEDFUNDS, signals that the Federal Reserve will maintain or increase rates to combat inflation. This monetary tightening cycle produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as real discount rates rise.
failure_condition: When both CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances, or the joint Rank IC of the inflation-rate pair is < 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly at levels above 2.5% annualized indicates sustained economic expansion and corporate earnings growth. This always-on trend signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market prices in continued expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11202.3B (2017 dollars) as of Q3 2005
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 level and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or periods of GDPC1 > 2.5% annualized growth fail to produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances.
